category_title: Students with off-task 'silly' behavior influenced by peer proximity and task purpose
minimal_context: Sammy is working independently in spelling class to place words in alphabetical order, write them three times each, and write sentences.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: For spelling class today, please place the words from your spelling list in alphabetical order, write them three times each, and make up sentences using the words.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the spelling list and begins making noises instead of writing the words in alphabetical order.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please keep working on your worksheet.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Engages in inappropriate talking during the academic activity rather than writing the words three times each.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to finish making up sentences using the words.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Exhibits completely off-task behavior, making loud noises, inappropriate gestures, and acting "silly" in the classroom.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: For spelling class today, please place the words from your spelling list in alphabetical order, write them three times each, and make up sentences using the words.

STUDENT: (Looks at the spelling list and begins making noises instead of writing the words in alphabetical order.)

TUTOR: Please keep working on your worksheet.

STUDENT: (Engages in inappropriate talking during the academic activity rather than writing the words three times each.)

TUTOR: You need to finish making up sentences using the words.

STUDENT: (Exhibits completely off-task behavior, making loud noises, inappropriate gestures, and acting "silly" in the classroom.)

task.content: Placed the words in alphabetical order, wrote them three times each, and made up sentences using the words

context.materials: Textbooks or worksheets, spelling list, tape recorder, sheet at his desk

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: During the analog condition, the student placed the words in alphabetical order, wrote them three times each, and made up sentences using the words
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Off-task behavior during academic activities; noise making or inappropriate talking during academic activities

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Making noises, inappropriate gestures, and “silly” behavior in the classroom
